mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high initial unemployment claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with high housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces inventory levels and boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with low industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial unemployment claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with low initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in housing starts below 1.0 million signals a weakening demand for housing that reduces equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with low housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 110 signals a strengthening supply chain that reduces inventory levels and boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days, but only when the federal funds rate is below 1.5%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high industrial production and low federal funds rate are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
